subject: LU-6602 llog: increase update llog chunk size

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

messages.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/32694/

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 
 
https://build.hpdd.intel.com/job/lustre-reviews/32694/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

With this patch I'm seeing:

[  760.060457] LustreError: 21179:0:(llog_osd.c:221:llog_osd_read_header()) sultan-MDT000f-osp-MDT0000: error readin
g log header from [0x5c000040f:0x1:0x0]: rc = -14
[  762.049217] Lustre: sultan-MDT0000: Will be in recovery for at least 7:30, or until 15 clients reconnect

Then in the recovery process it continues recovery even when everything reconnets.

[  857.740455] Lustre: sultan-MDT0000: Denying connection for new client 39f0eeda-6508-874c-98b3-f2b7f28b0c2e(at 10.37.248.124@o2ib1), waiting for 15 known clients (15 recovered, 0 in progress, and 0 evicted) to recover in 20:54
[  894.733780] Lustre: sultan-MDT0000: Denying connection for new client 39f0eeda-6508-874c-98b3-f2b7f28b0c2e(at 10.37.248.124@o2ib1), waiting for 15 known clients (15 recovered, 0 in progress, and 0 evicted) to recover in 20:18

Then once the recovery timer expires it keeps going.

[ 1801.038005] Lustre: sultan-MDT0000: Client sultan-MDT0008-mdtlov_UUID (at 10.37.248.162@o2ib1) reconnecting, waiting for 15 clients in recovery for 5:11
[ 1801.051790] Lustre: Skipped 1198 previous similar messages
[ 2115.922937] Lustre: sultan-MDT0000: Denying connection for new client 39f0eeda-6508-874c-98b3-f2b7f28b0c2e(at 10.37.248.124@o2ib1), waiting for 15 known clients (15 recovered, 0 in progress, and 0 evicted) to recover in 21188505:16
[ 2115.943651] Lustre: Skipped 13 previous similar messages

Patch Set 1:

James:

"With this patch I'm seeing:
[ 760.060457] LustreError: 21179:0:(llog_osd.c:221:llog_osd_read_header()) sultan-MDT000f-osp-MDT0000: error readin g log header from [0x5c000040f:0x1:0x0]: rc = -14 [ 762.049217] Lustre: sultan-MDT0000: Will be in recovery for at least 7:30, or until 15 clients reconnect"

Hmm, this might because you some update logs are already created before this patch increase the update logs. i.e. you might not meet this problem if you start from a reformatted FS. Anyway, I will fix this, thanks for testing.

Still getting the same errors.

Patch Set 2:

James: Could you please tell me how to reproduce the problem? thanks

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Probably the best solution is to always start by trying to read the larger llog header size (which will succeed for new llog files, and old llog files that have more than a few blocks of data), and if -EFAULT is returned and hdr_size > LLOG_MIN_CHUNK_SIZE, then retry with hdr_size = LLOG_MIN_CHUNK_SIZE:

Ok, I will go this way.

It would be great if there was a test for this if the patch is refreshed. Maybe a dail_loc to write a recovery log with the old 8KB size, then recover without the fail_loc and check that the new code still works.

Just to confirm, this change only affects DNE OUT recovery logs for now? It would be great to add this to the commit message of the patch is refreshed.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 5:

Yes, Only for DNE update log.

parents.subject: LU-3534 osp: transfer updates with bulk RPC

commit.subject: LU-6602 llog: increase update llog chunk size
commit.message:
LU-6602 llog: increase update llog chunk size

Increase DNE update llog chunk size from 8KB to 32KB, so
one cross-MDT operation can includes more update records,
and it can create striped directory with more stripe count.

Signed-off-by: wang di <di.wang@intel.com>
Change-Id: I01d1435f1249c3048ac0a412bdb53f710e6e98b3
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/15161
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
